name: Hannah
text: I went to this concentration camp with my school on 18 March and that was this lovely tour guide Tina she had an Australian accent in case there was two Tina’s  She was very direct and straight to the point and genuinely seemed interested in our answers and questions and didn’t drag things out. She ...

name: Himanshu Jindal
text:
A Chilling Reminder of What Humanity Must Never Become…

Visiting this concentration camp had been on my bucket list for a long time — and standing there in person was far more powerful and unsettling than anything I’d ever read or watched.

This place is a masterclass in deception, cruelty, and the...

name: Sylvie Llanes
text:
We took the public transport. .the entry is free, but highly recommend getting the audio guide for self guided tour.
It was a rainy day, but it added depth to the ordeal that many people experienced in this dreadful place.
Make sure you have great walking shoes and be prepare to walk a lot.

name: Marie Yorke
text: The sad history behind this concentration camp is unreal and well worth a visit if you want to hear the true stories of what the Nazzis did to the Jews, in 1939 to 45.

name: Wan Lin Qin
text: The tour guide went above and beyond in telling us stories about the history, political climate and historical events around the Holocaust and how they echoe in today's world. You can tell he is truly well-read and passionate about justice and the meaning for sharing this difficult part of humanity—...

name: Bengü Su
text:
It was a sad but educational experience,it is
better to come with a tour and a guide.

name: Alejandro Deulofeu Fleites
text: Amazing place and amazing experience with our tour guide, he has amazing knowledge and he is really passionated in sharing it with the crew; very interesting facts and very detailed information all the way.

name: Jude Weddell
text: We have all heard about the atrocities during WW11 ,but this place is a real awakening to just how cruel humans can be to other humans.   To see the actual showers where people were gassed and the ovens used to incinerate was shocking.   To read the stories of both the interred and 'staff' again too...

name: Filipe Pires
text: Miguel is a fantastic guide with a real passion for 20th-century history, especially the events leading up to WWII. What I really liked was how he connected so many different parts of the story, giving us a broader understanding of how the world ended up on the path that led to the war and the atroc...

name: Patricia Uiterwyk
text: Our tour was great from beginning to end. We were able to learn about historic events around WWII while commuting with no stress to the Sachsenhausen Memorial. Our guide (Miquel) was kind and informational. Compassionate and easy to understand, we really liked the way we were on time and kept at a c...

name: Sandy Kerr
text: Amazing free museum. It is hard to describe by the nature of what this place is but it is well worth a visit very thought provoking it would be that it would be my most recommended thing to do whilst in the area.

name: Katie Clarke
text:
It's definitely a place and tour you need to do while in Berlin.
The tour guide was very informative and knowledgeable.
Place was super busy, but due to the size, I was still able to see everything and learn about the tragic events, punishment, and killings that went on.
Really hits home and makes y...

name: Dr. Zoidberg PhD.
text: Matias was our tour guide this afternoon. Excellent guide. I would 100% ask for his your if you're an English speaker. The weather was a little rough today, but he have us a lot of information and even helped us get back to the train station!

name: Edwin Luckers
text: Very interesting to see this horrible side of history. There are moments when you realize what people were capable of. I hope all will learn and remember these dark years and all can live in peace..

name: Karen Malone
text: Thanks so much Matias for a great tour and how  knowledgeable you are.  Would recommend this tour.

name: Russell Wood
text: It was an excellent tour. Our guide was Miguel. He has obviously spent years researching the history and shared it in an interesting way. I would definitely recommend.

name: andres felipe Chinchilla Ballesteros
text: The experience and sensation is hard to feel there, dificult to know about how the people were treated there.

name: Ralph Gonzalez
text: This was a great tour, included a quick lesson on public transportation in Berlin. We met our Guide, Miguel and thought what are the odds of running into a tour guide from Orange County. He was amazing and very knowledgeable. I highly recommend asking for Miguel as your tour guide.

name: DeAnn Martin
text: Our tour guide, Xavier, was excellent. He was knowledgeable & kind & went out of his way to include everyone in the conversation. He answered questions patiently. This tour is painful. You should leave feeling the weight of this awful time period & the loss of so many lives at the hands of evil. We ...

name: justin dillon
text:
A very important site in history. Lewis was very knowledgeable and helpful with our group. Helped us get tickets for the train and had great directions for our journey after.
His knowledge of the site memorial and history made this worth the trip and we highly recommend it.
Hottest day of the year a...

name: Alec R.
text:
Words can describe it, but it's better to see it in person to get the full immersion...
I will not post pics of the camp so you can discover the horrors by yourself.
It was the first concentration camp, where it all began.
The entry is free, but a guided tour is recommended if you want to learn the ...

name: Chris R
text: Book this tour. You don’t want to miss out on this opportunity. We learned so much about Sachsenhauser concentration camp and the historical context of how it and the concentration camp system during the  Second World War came about and  why. Georgia , our guide, was great. She was so knowledgeable ...

name: Mike Adelman
text: Canadian Chris was a superb tour guide!! He was so knowledgeable and passionate to share this tragic history of Sachsenhausen. He shared great detail and was incredibly responsive to our questions. The walk to the camp was incredibly impactful. He is an absolute value add to every tour he gives!!

name: Paul Schembri
text:
Great tour. Sebastian was our guide. Super informative, great ability to deliver the story with compassion, with empathy all while trying to connect it to 2024.

Would highly recommend a day doing this tour.

name: Elisabeth Malik
text: Very informative and thought provoking. Our guide, Sebastian was excellent. I highly recommend this tour.

name: Wim De Wulf
text: We want to thank our tour guide today for an excellent tour at Saskenhausen concentration camp bringing loads of information concise, to the point, relevant, interconnected with world wide insights and all that with a lot of passion! One of the best guides we have had in a very long time!!! Well don...

name: Caitlin Jenkins
text: (Canadian) Chris' tour of Sachenhausen was comprehensive and thorough. He explained the dreadful history in a digestible way so that I feel confident accurately sharing the story with others. This tour was a highlight of my trip to Berlin and has given me a greater perspective of these tragic events...

name: Luke Law
text: Our tour guide Miguel was incredibly knowledgeable and very passionate about the history of not only the camp but also Germany. His facts and stories truly opened our eyes to the reality and tragedy of what happened here, I could not of asked for a better guide. Highly recommend this tour and hope t...

name: Georgina Harden
text: Great tour, our guide was extremely knowledgeable even before we got to the camp and was able to tell us lots of interesting information. The guide was able to help us all with transport too, thank you Miguel!

name: Shaun Barron
text:
Very informative tour our guide was very good.
Lots of very interesting facts we didn't know.
Could have spent lot more time, but we'll worth the money spent.

name: Anthony Vaughan
text: My partner and I went on the Sachsenhausen concentration camp tour on Tuesday 26th March. Our tour guide, Sebastian, was fantastic from the start. He made sure we understood where we were going, what the tour was and what it wasn't, and most importantly explaining the reasons why a place like Sachse...

name: Roger Hatten
text: Sachsenhausen is a very sad and neccesary part of history to be understood. From Berlin it is nearly a 2 hour trip to get there and same return. 5 hours incl travel was not enough to do it justice. Get your train planner out and go at your own pace.

name: NBR North Brisbane Rentals
text: A very informative and interesting tour. Our guide, Miguel, was incredible - extensive knowledge, very passionate and energetic. He was very helpful and friendly. Thank you for a memorable tour.

name: Tracy Robinson
text: Our tour guide (originally from USA) was very informative, knowledgeable and extremely passionate about his job, he made the whole experience come to life. Only regret was I didn’t get his name.

name: always smile
text: I recently had the opportunity to visit this historical camp, and I must say it was a unique and captivating experience. It was on my bucket list and I always waited to visit it here I must recommend visiting this place especially for a history lover💓😍

name: Sarah Mai Kavanagh
text: George was a brilliant tour guide making it a personal experience from start to finish. Attention grabbing and engaging throughout. In the space of a few hours I learnt more history about the Holocaust and WW2 than I have in my life.  Also touched on with what is unfortunately happening in the world...

name: always smile
text:
Amazing experience to see the one more chapter in the history of the World 🌎
Must go place for traveller 👍😍
I highly recommend each of the history lovers in the World to see this place and please add it into your bucket list now 😍👍❤️

name: Ashley Braden
text: Miguel, was an incredible historian! What a treat to land such a knowledgeable guide for this tour. Thank you for the passion and detail, from the start in Berlin through to saying goodbye.

name: marina marinelli
text: The tour of the Sachsenhausen concentration camp is emotionally challenging and unthinkable to do without a guide. Our guide, Luca, was very thorough and provided numerous details that made the visit even more interesting. Moreover, he also assisted us with directions for our return to Berlin using ...

name: Billy MacDougall
text: If you ever visit Berlin. Visit this as well. Was worth it. To hear what went on and to see the conditions they was made to live/work. Was a real eye opening for me. We went with a tour guide. And was in a group with George our tour guide and he was great.  Knew his stuff. Took time to answer everyo...
